notes: Central figure of the fourfold Gospel witness, teacher, healer, and risen Lord.

Beloved witness at the supper, the cross, and the empty tomb.

Birth city of Jesus and city of David memory.

Desert testing ground after the baptism.
Village of the wedding sign and later healing word.
Major Galilean base of ministry, healing, and teaching.
Waters of calling, storms, and miracles.
Fishing-region setting tied to provision and healing.
Site of Peter's confession and the gates-of-hell saying.
Village of Lazarus, Martha, and Mary near Jerusalem.
City tied to mercy on the road and Zacchaeus in the tree.
City of entry, teaching, trial, crucifixion, and resurrection witness.
Charged setting for cleansing, teaching, and confrontation.

Hill of approach, discourse, prayer, and departure.
Garden of prayer, sorrow, and arrest.
Place of the Skull where Jesus is crucified.
Village of post-resurrection recognition on the road.
Poolside setting of the long-disabled man's healing.

Burial place transformed into the sign of resurrection victory.

Gold, frankincense, and myrrh brought to the newborn king.
Lowly cradle of the nativity scene.
Stone vessels transformed into the setting of Jesus' first sign.
Provision symbols multiplied in the wilderness feeding.
Precious vessel broken in an act of anointing before the burial.
Victory branches spread during the triumphal entry.
Betrayal payment that becomes a haunting passion emblem.
Service implements of the foot-washing scene.
Mocking royal emblem placed on Jesus in the passion.
Garment not torn but cast over by lot at the cross.
Central instrument of the Gospel passion climax.
Folded grave linens left behind in the tomb.
Barrier-stone moved from the tomb in resurrection imagery.
Gabriel's message to Mary announcing the conception of Jesus.
Meeting of Mary and Elisabeth with blessing and song.
Birth of Jesus in humble circumstances.
The shepherds' angel-announced visit to the newborn child.
Eastern sages arrive under the sign of the star.
Holy family's escape from Herod's threat.
Public inauguration scene in the Jordan.
Visible sign of divine approval at the baptism.
Heavenly declaration over the beloved Son.
Forty-day testing of Jesus in the desert.
Fishers are summoned to become fishers of men.
Foundational mountain teaching on kingdom life.
Blessed-sayings that define upside-down kingdom values.
Model prayer placed at the heart of Jesus' instruction.
Signature Gospel proclamation of divine reign breaking in.
Seed-scattering image for hearing and fruitfulness.
Tiny beginning that becomes a sheltering kingdom image.
Compressed image of difficult but life-giving discipleship.
Purifying touch miracle early in the Galilean ministry.
Forgiveness and healing converge in the lowered-mat scene.
Distance-healing tied to remarkable faith.
Wind and waves obey Jesus' rebuke.
Sea-crossing miracle joined to Peter's wavering steps.
Loaves-and-fishes provision sign for the multitude.
Second wilderness feeding miracle with basket imagery.
Mountain revelation of glory before chosen disciples.
Exorcism climax that sends demons into the herd.

simple_name: Woman with an Issue of Blood
notes: Healing by touch on the way to Jairus' house.
Mercy parable centered on the wounded traveler and unexpected neighbor.
Homecoming parable of squandered inheritance and lavish welcome.
Search-and-recovery image of divine joy.
Barn-building parable against hoarding life.
Prayer contrast between self-righteousness and humility.
Salvation comes to the house of the tree-climbing tax collector.
